finding: During the ATM-GOV-0168..0179 dogfood sequence, several governance steps were still easy to stall on: prompt-scoped next could route broad follow-up work to generic guidance instead of the intended plan/card set; unowned runtime receipts were reported as L3 active work even when unrelated; claim/steward/runner-sync actor identity mismatches caused reruns; and dirty WIP ownership was not obvious enough for agents to distinguish active Cursor work from stale generated residue.
expectedBehavior: ATM should distinguish plan-scoped continuation work from unrelated global queue state, classify generated/runtime residue separately from actionable foreign WIP, surface the owning actor/session for each blocker, and offer a one-command recovery path for actor mismatch, stale receipt cleanup, and safe continuation after another agent closes.
evidenceOrRepro: Observed in the 2026-07-18/2026-07-19 GOV auto-batch dogfood: Cursor-owned 0168/0180 work required manual avoidance; runner-sync first failed on actor mismatch before rerun with ATM_ACTOR_ID=codex-gov-auto-batch; later `atm next` reported unowned `.atm/history/evidence/*.runner-sync-receipt.json` as active L3 work even after the target plan was complete.
followUp: Open a GOV usability card for plan-scoped continuation routing, WIP provenance display, stale runtime receipt classification, and automatic recovery hints for actor mismatch and unrelated dirty WIP.
